title: Ship class document
description: A hull's deck plan plus the cargo characteristics M3 trading needs. Derived from the Gleam decoders in server/src/dh_server/shipclass.gleam (ship_class_decoder/cargo_decoder/handling_decoder) and server/src/dh_server/deckplan.gleam (decoder, embedded at the top level via decode.then) — those decoders are the source of truth; this schema is documentation and a typo-catcher, not a substitute for them. additionalProperties is false at every object level even though the decoders themselves ignore unknown fields, precisely so this schema can catch typos the decoders would silently swallow.

walkable.description: One string per row, top to bottom. Alphabet: '.' hull/void, '#' generic single floor (admits both decks), 'L' lower-deck only, 'U' upper-deck only, '2' two stacked floors (no vertical connection), 'B' between-level (one floor connecting both decks). Row count and each row's length must match grid.height/grid.width, and there must be a console of kind "helm" (enforced by the Gleam decoder's validate step, not by this schema).

spawn_tile.description: [x, y] tile where arriving characters appear (the airlock end).

capacity.description: Hold size in cargo units. Must be >= 0 (enforced by the Gleam decoder's validate step, not by this schema).

handling.description: How cargo physically gets aboard: break-bulk hulls load by robot stevedores anywhere; container hulls need a station crane and never open their holds.

dock_port_orientation.description: Optional field (decode.optional_field, default pi/2). This hull's docking-port outward normal in its own frame (ship-local radians, 0 = nose/+x). pi/2 = port flank (side-on mooring, the default). Combined with a station berth's orientation to fix the moored heading (issue #14).

deck.description: Split-level rendering metadata: draw this room's label only on the given deck's view. Omitted = both decks.

kind.description: Free-form in the decoder (e.g. "helm"/"cargo" are conventions, not constraints); left open here. At least one console of kind "helm" is required by the Gleam decoder's validate step, not by this schema.
